methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Lila Sciences is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Geoffrey von Maltzahn and Molly Gibson that has raised $200M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 11 peer companies—3 public-market anchors (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent, predominantly at Series B stage.

Two valuation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis, using the $200M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-later-stage progression, implies a $400M valuation. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $805M implied value, with the peer set ranging from $100M to $3.3B. The public-comp anchoring method (3-ticker P/S median of 7.4x in Biotech & Health) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target.

The triangulated valuation range spans $340M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $602M based on the two applicable methods. Key uncertainties include the relatively wide dispersion among peer valuations, the stage heterogeneity of the comparable set (predominantly Series B vs. target's Series A), and the unavailability of revenue data to cross-check against public market multiples.
